summary.why: The new attribution contract was available but agent-facing prime and onboarding guidance neither encouraged corrections nor reconciled explicit identities with privacy rules.
summary.how: Updated compact and full prime, onboard, log help, and MCP schema guidance to prefer automatic attribution and reserve --who for intentional full-set replacement using publishable identities.

notes: Kept this progressive: agents usually do nothing because Git attribution is automatic. Explicit steering appears only for pairing, shared work, bots, or correction, and warns that any --who value replaces the entire automatic set.
